title: In /Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers/claude-config, verify branch/commit existence, whether commit 1c8023c7 is on feature/phase0-handoff-pipeline, and whether there is evidence it was pushed. Use local git evidence only unless remote check is necessary. Return concise findings. Do not edit files.
summary_text: ### Findings (ordered by severity) 1. **[BLOCKING] `kernel-launch-quorum` does not prove codex launch-preflight success despite contract requiring durable codex preflight evidence.** In [`scripts/lib/kernel-launch-quorum.sh`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-launch-quorum.sh), the launch preflight path runs `run_codex_prompt_check` (prompt-existence check) and only executes GLM and specialist lanes (`run_glm_lane`, `run_specialist_lane`), so the script itself never records/forces a codex launch-preflight success event in this preflight function; this diverges from the contract’s “codex + glm + specialist” preflight proof requirement in [`docs/kernel/kernel-working-orchestration-contract-v1.md`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/kernel/kernel-working-orchestration-contract-v1.md). 2. **[P1] Specialist-quorum verification is under-specified in health/consensus (non-baseline providers can satisfy quorum).** `kernel-runtime-health.sh` and `kernel-consensus-evidence.sh` count specialist success by “all providers except codex/glm” (`.key != "codex" and .key != "glm"`), so any extra provider success (including non-contract lanes such as xAI-like keys if present) can satisfy specialist proof where contract defines specialists as `gemini-cli|cursor-cli|copilot-cli` only. This appears in [`scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh) and [`scripts/lib/kernel-consensus-evidence.sh`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-consensus-evidence.sh), and weakens contract fidelity. 3. **[P2] `record-from-runtime` can mint runtime-backed consensus without canonical-chain integrity checks beyond bootstrap+ledger.** Runtime-backed consensus in [`scripts/lib/kernel-consensus-evidence.sh`] only checks bootstrap + provider counters; it does not validate the workspace/compact/compact-session chain before approval. That is weaker than the contract’s “canonical chain” requirement (`bootstrap receipt → runtime ledger → compact artifact → workspace receipt → consensus receipt`) in the orchestration contract. 4. **[P2] New tests are mostly synthetic/unit-level and do not prove dynamic specialist selection and full end-to-end orchestration in live mode.** - `test-kernel-launch-working-orchestration.sh` forces provider set (`KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_PROVIDERS_CSV="codex,glm,gemini-cli"`) and stubs executables, then checks ledger counts and scheduler terminal state. - `test-kernel-runtime-health.sh` directly writes ledger/receipt fixtures and toggles states, proving evaluator logic, not full lane orchestration. - `test-kernel-working-orchestration-sim.sh` verifies split-root path recovery and a consensus record, but ledger/receipt/compact are pre-populated manually. Together, these do not exercise a real dynamic specialist choice or prove provider-actual execution artifacts for non-happy-paths at runtime (`codex-kernel-guard`, bootstrap, quorum, consensus loop) in one complete chain. ### Residual risks (if these are accepted as non-blocking) - A run with stale or out-of-band ledger/provider entries could still report healthy/approved while not reflecting actual current preflight execution quality. - Missing verification of compact/workspace/consensus linkage in consensus recording can let a run pass even when live continuity artifacts drift from bootstrap/ledger state. - Dynamic-provider fallback behavior (`AUTO` specialist selection in `codex-kernel-guard.sh`) is not covered by the tested scenarios, so selection regressions can slip into production despite passing current tests.
